hero.name:
BALA AGATHEESAN
D K
hero.subtitle: Malware Analyst · Security Researcher · AI/DS Engineer
hero.bio: I analyze and reverse what others build to break things. 25+ real Windows malware samples dissected — viruses, worms, RATs, droppers. Windows internals, PE format, persistence mechanisms. MITRE ATT&CK mappings.

profile.title: Behind the Terminal
profile.sub: Bridging threat intelligence with machine learning.
paragraphs: B.Tech student in Artificial Intelligence & Data Science at Knowledge Institute of Technology, Salem — CGPA 8.35. My academic path diverged early into the darker corridors of cybersecurity.
Trained hands-on malware analysis. Analyzed 25–30+ real Windows malware samples across five families.

I work where malware meets intelligence — reverse engineering binaries, mapping adversary TTPs to MITRE ATT&CK, and understanding the precise API hooks and injection mechanisms that compromise Windows kernels.
JLPT N5 certified. JLPT N3 is the next milestone.

projects.title: Projects
projects.sub: Fully functional applications and automated tooling built with code.

list.title: College Lab Management
list.body: A digital resource orchestration system built to streamline inventory tracking, lab allocations, hardware maintenance logs, and schedules for college academic labs.

list.title: Nearby College Event Finder
list.body: A localized, geo-aware web application mapping technical symposia, academic hackathons, and cultural events hosted across nearby partner institutions in real-time.

list.title: Emergency AI-Assistant
list.body: An AI-powered emergency assistance solution that detects road accidents, tracks the victim's location using GPS, and identifies nearby hospitals for quick medical support. By automating accident detection and emergency response, it helps reduce reporting delays and improves road safety.

operations.title: Operations Sandbox
operations.sub: Dissecting real-world malware samples inside a simulated SOC environment.
operations.diagnosticsReadyText: // Diagnostics ready. Select a malware sample and press run.

usbWorm.name: Worm.Propagate.USB
usbWorm.staticNotes: Flagged 14 critical IAT imports. High probability of removable media scanning (T1091).
dynamicLogs: 00:01.95 - [WARN] Detected drive type modification: Drive E:\\ (REMOVABLE)

fileDrops.desc: Replicated worm executable masquerading as a system file.

mitreTactics.name: Replication Through Removable Media
mitreTactics.desc: Self-propagates by dropping replica binaries and malicious autorun files on accessible external flash drives.

mitreTactics.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
mitreTactics.desc: Adds payload path to the HKCU Run registry key to trigger automatic startup upon Windows logon.

mitreTactics.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
mitreTactics.desc: Establishes Command and Control (C2) communication via standard HTTP requests to exfiltrate logs.

dropperPE.name: Dropper.Win32.PE
dropperPE.staticNotes: High entropy score indicates compression or encryption. Uses VirtualAlloc and WriteProcessMemory for process injection.
dynamicLogs: 00:01.30 - [AUDIT] Searching application resources for encrypted payload...
00:02.90 - [INFO] Decrypting embedded payload into allocated space...
00:03.45 - [WARN] Attempting hollow process injection into svchost.exe (PID: 1204)...
00:04.60 - [ALERT] Remote thread created inside svchost.exe (Process Hollowing)
00:06.40 - [INFO] Terminating initial dropper process (PID: 4920) to evade detection.
00:06.90 - [SUCCESS] Sandbox run completed. Injection payload logged.

fileDrops.desc: Injected dynamically linked library containing C2 agent logic.

registryChanges.desc: System-wide startup hook to launch DLL load.

mitreTactics.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
mitreTactics.desc: Employs heavy byte-level encryption of its internal sub-payload, generating a high entropy rate to mask code structures.

mitreTactics.name: Process Injection: Process Hollowing
mitreTactics.desc: Launches a suspended legitimate system utility (svchost.exe) and overwrites its memory space with malicious C2 commands.

mitreTactics.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
mitreTactics.desc: Injects startup persistence hooks in the LocalMachine hive (HKLM) requiring admin privileges.

ratDarkLoris.name: RAT.DarkLoris.AI
ratDarkLoris.staticNotes: Packed binary. Flagged API calls for scanning local computer names and system resources for target categorization.
dynamicLogs: 00:01.35 - [AUDIT] Gathering system profiles (Host, User, OS info)...
00:01.80 - [INFO] System Profile: HOST="WKSTN-920", USER="Administrator", OS="Windows 11 Enterprise"
00:02.25 - [WARN] Searching for security software running in memory...
00:02.80 - [AUDIT] Evasion: Antivirus software check completed (No AV active in sandbox)
00:03.90 - [ALERT] Command Executed: netsh advfirewall firewall add rule...
00:06.30 - [ALERT] Network Connection: Outbound to 93.184.216.34:443 (SSL/TLS Encryption Enabled)
00:06.90 - [SUCCESS] Sandbox run completed. C2 payload connection active.

fileDrops.desc: Raw binary log collecting user keystrokes.

registryChanges.desc: Disables screensaver security limits to sustain user state tracking.

mitreTactics.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify System Firewall
mitreTactics.desc: Disables or overrides network filtering limits by injecting firewall allow policies via command console.

mitreTactics.name: Input Capture: Keylogging
mitreTactics.desc: Monitors user typing behaviors, storing active keystrokes in a local cache (keylog.dat) for structured uploading.

mitreTactics.name: Exfiltration Over Command and Control Channel
mitreTactics.desc: Transmits compressed target configurations and sensitive files back to the adversary host using encrypted TLS sessions.

Special interest in Tokyo-based cyber firms (building toward N3 Japanese capability). If you are looking for an analyst who reverses binaries instead of reading slides, let's talk.

about.header: --- Telemetry Dossier: Bala Agatheesan D K ---
lines: Track     : Artificial Intelligence & Data Science (B.Tech Pre-Final Year)
Academy   : Knowledge Institute of Technology (CGPA: 8.35)
Training  : Professionally mentored under K7 Computing researchers (K7 Academy).
Rank      : Awarded TOP grade by professional threat researchers in the program.
Samples   : 25-30+ real malware samples fully dissected (viruses, worms, RATs, droppers).
Japanese  : JLPT N5 certified. Studying for N3 next to target Japanese cybersecurity roles.
[Analysis] Static/Dynamic analysis, PE structural analysis, Registry forensics
[Tools] IDA Freeware, x32dbg, OllyDbg, FakeNet-NG, Wireshark, Process Monitor
[Threat Intel] MITRE ATT&CK tactics, Windows internals & API Hooking, C2 behaviors
[AI & Code] Python, PyTorch, SQL, Power BI, Java, C, Django, Git/GitHub
1. [Worm.Removable.USB] - USB Worm Propagation Behavioral Analysis
- Static phase: analyzed PE structure, flagged 14 suspicious API imports.
- Dynamic phase: traced registry run key HKCU\\Run\\SysHelper, and FakeNet C2 DNS requests.
2. [Malware-Archive] - 20+ Real Samples Catalog (Worm, Virus, RAT, Dropper)
3. [AI-Threat-Detection] - Adversarial ML and Deep Learning-based Classification
• T1091: Replication Through Removable Media (Removable USB worms)
• T1547: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution (HKCU\\Run registry drop)
• T1012: Query Registry (Detecting security controls/AV systems)
• T1132: Data Encoding (Obfuscating strings inside PE loader)
• T1071: Application Layer Protocol (C2 network beaconing via custom domains)
LinkedIn : linkedin.com/in/agatheesan
GitHub : github.com/agatheesan
Email : agatheesan@email.com
Status    : Open for malware analysis roles & research collaborations
